note: Imports per-namespace topic indexes (ca-topic-index.json / kr-topic-index.json / eu-topic-index.json).

topics.title: Personal Data Breach Notification and Communication
topics.summary: GDPR's two breach-response duties: notification to the supervisory authority within 72 hours of awareness (Art. 33) and communication to data subjects without undue delay when high-risk (Art. 34). Includes content requirements and the high-risk carve-outs.
answer_cautions: 72-hour rule is for SA only; subject-facing communication (Art. 34) is 'without undue delay' and gated by high-risk.
Art. 34(3) carve-outs (rendered unintelligible / risk eliminated / disproportionate effort) replace individual communication, not eliminate the SA notification.

topics.title: Lawful Basis for Processing and Conditions for Consent
topics.summary: GDPR Art. 6 lawful bases (consent, contract, legal obligation, vital interests, public task, legitimate interests) and the strict consent conditions of Art. 7 (and Art. 8 for children).
answer_cautions: Recitals are interpretive aids, not binding. Cite the underlying Article for binding rules.
Legitimate-interest balancing (6(1)(f)) requires a documented assessment; cannot be the default for direct marketing across all member states.

topics.title: Data Subject Rights (Access / Rectification / Erasure / Restriction / Portability / Objection)
topics.summary: Chapter III rights: access (Art. 15), rectification (Art. 16), erasure (Art. 17), restriction (Art. 18), portability (Art. 20), objection (Art. 21).
answer_cautions: Each right has different exemptions and grounds — do not flatten into one generic 'data subject right'.
One-month default response window (Art. 12(3)), extendable by two months for complex requests.

topics.title: Special Categories of Personal Data (Sensitive Data)
topics.summary: GDPR Art. 9 prohibition on processing special categories (health, biometric, genetic, racial/ethnic, political, religious, trade-union, sex life/orientation) and the ten exhaustive derogations.
answer_cautions: Art. 9 derogations are exhaustive — member-state law may add restrictions but cannot expand grounds.
Explicit consent under Art. 9(2)(a) is stricter than Art. 6 consent; document the explicit nature.

topics.title: International Data Transfers (Chapter V)
topics.summary: Transfer of personal data to third countries: adequacy decisions (Art. 45), appropriate safeguards including SCCs and BCRs (Art. 46-47), and derogations for specific situations (Art. 49). Built on the Art. 44 general principle.
answer_cautions: Schrems II (C-311/18) requires a transfer impact assessment even when SCCs are used; mention but do not over-claim.
Art. 49 derogations are narrowly construed and not for routine transfers.

topics.title: Data Protection Impact Assessment and Prior Consultation
topics.summary: When a DPIA is required (Art. 35) and the obligation to consult the supervisory authority before processing where residual high risk remains (Art. 36).
answer_cautions: Each member state's SA may publish a 'must DPIA' / 'never DPIA' list — local SA guidance overrides generic mapping.
DPIA is a process, not a one-off form; review when processing changes materially.

topics.title: Automated Individual Decision-Making, Including Profiling
topics.summary: GDPR Art. 22 right not to be subject to a decision based solely on automated processing (including profiling) producing legal or similarly significant effects, with the three statutory exceptions.
answer_cautions: Art. 22 applies to decisions based 'solely' on automated processing — human-in-the-loop changes the analysis.
Three statutory exceptions (contract necessity / EU or member-state law / explicit consent) are exhaustive.

topics.title: Enforcement, Remedies, Liability, and Penalties
topics.summary: Right to lodge a complaint (Art. 77), right to compensation (Art. 82), and the two-tier administrative fines structure of Art. 83 (up to 10/20 million EUR or 2/4% of global turnover).
answer_cautions: Art. 83 has TWO fine tiers — distinguish 10M/2% (lower-tier infringements) from 20M/4% (upper-tier infringements).
Compensation under Art. 82 is judicial; administrative fines under Art. 83 are imposed by the SA.

topics.title: Personal Information Breach Notification and Reporting
topics.summary: When and how a 개인정보처리자 must notify affected data subjects and report to PIPC/KISA after a personal information leak. Statute fixes 'without delay'; the Enforcement Decree fixes 72-hour outer limits and the >=1000 subjects / sensitive-data / external-access trigger for PIPC reporting.
answer_cautions: 본법 '지체 없이' 와 시행령 '72시간' 의 관계: 시행령은 outer limit, '지체 없이' 가 더 빠른 시점이면 그것이 기준.
PIPC 신고 trigger 3가지 (1000명 이상 / 민감·고유식별정보 / 외부 불법접근) 충족 여부를 즉시 평가.

topics.title: Consent and Lawful Collection of Personal Information
topics.summary: PIPA's consent-led model for collecting and using personal information, including consent contents (Art. 15), method (Art. 22), and collection limits (Art. 16).
answer_cautions: 동의 외 처리 근거 (제15조 제1항 각호) 도 제공 — '동의' 만 강조하지 말 것.
민감정보·고유식별정보는 별도 동의 (제23조·제24조) 필요 — 일반 동의로 대체 불가.

topics.title: Data Subject Rights (Access / Correction / Deletion / Restriction / Portability)
topics.summary: 정보주체의 권리 — 열람 (제35조), 정정·삭제 (제36조), 처리정지 (제37조), 전송 요구 (제35조의2), 그리고 권리행사 절차 (제38조).
answer_cautions: 권리별 처리 기한 및 거부 사유는 시행령에 별도 규정 — 시행령 미인용 시 답변 불완전.
전송 요구 (제35조의2) 는 GDPR portability 와 유사하지만 시행 범위·대상이 다름.

topics.title: Sensitive Information and Unique Identifiers
topics.summary: 민감정보 (제23조), 고유식별정보 (제24조), 주민등록번호 (제24조의2) 의 처리 제한과 별도 동의·법적 근거 요건.
answer_cautions: 민감정보·고유식별정보 처리는 일반 동의로 갈음할 수 없음 — 별도 동의 또는 법령 근거 필수.
주민등록번호는 제24조의2 의 별도 더 엄격한 처리 제한 적용.

topics.title: Cross-Border Transfer of Personal Information
topics.summary: 개인정보의 국외 이전 (제28조의8) 의 5개 근거와 중지 명령 (제28조의9). 2023년 개정으로 적정성 결정·인증·계약 등 다양화.
answer_cautions: 5개 근거 (동의 / 법률·조약 / 표준계약·인증 / 적정성 결정 / 기타 정보주체 권익 보호조치) 중 어느 것에 해당하는지 명시.
PIPC 가 중지 명령 (제28조의9) 을 발동할 수 있는 사유에 주의.

topics.title: Personal Information Impact Assessment
topics.summary: 개인정보 영향평가 (제33조) 의 대상 (공공기관의 일정 규모 개인정보파일), 절차, PIPC 제출 의무.
answer_cautions: 현행 PIPA 영향평가 의무는 공공기관 중심. 민간 자율 PIA 와 구분.
대상 규모 기준은 시행령에 위임 — 시행령 확인 필수.

topics.title: Rights Regarding Automated Decision-Making
topics.summary: 자동화된 결정에 대한 정보주체의 권리 (제37조의2). 2023년 신설, GDPR Art. 22 와 유사한 거부·설명 요구권.
answer_cautions: 거부권·설명요구권의 발동 요건과 제외 사유 (계약 체결·이행, 법령상 의무 등) 를 명확히 구분.
GDPR Art. 22 와 1:1 매칭 아님 — 비교 답변 시 차이 명시.

topics.title: Enforcement, Administrative Sanctions, and Civil Liability
topics.summary: PIPC 의 시정조치 (제64조), 과징금 (제64조의2, 2023년 매출 3% 한도로 강화), 형사 벌칙 (제70조 이하), 민사 손해배상 (제39조).
answer_cautions: 2023년 개정 과징금 (전체 매출 3% 한도) 와 구 과태료의 차이 명확히.
PIPC 결정 (administrative) 과 법원 판결 (judicial) 을 혼동하지 말 것.

topics.title: Notice at Collection and Privacy Disclosures
topics.summary: When and how a business must disclose categories, purposes, sale/share status, retention, and rights at or before collection.
answer_cautions: Do not substitute an OAG FAQ for the statute or CPPA regulations.
Separate CCPA notice-at-collection duties from CalOPPA privacy-policy posting duties.
comparative_hooks: PIPA privacy policy and collection notice rules

topics.title: Opt Out of Sale/Share and GPC
topics.summary: Consumer opt-out rights for sale/share, opt-out links, opt-out preference signals, and frictionless processing.
answer_cautions: Do not call enforcement settlements judicial precedent.
Check whether the issue is sale, share, or both.

topics.title: Sensitive Personal Information and Limit Use
topics.summary: Definition and limit-use framework for sensitive personal information, including notice and risk-assessment overlap.
answer_cautions: Medical or health-related data may also require CMIA analysis.
Private-right-of-action analysis is narrower than general CCPA compliance analysis.

topics.title: Data Minimization and Purpose Limitation
topics.summary: Reasonable, necessary, and proportionate collection/use/retention under the CCPA and CPPA regulations.
answer_cautions: Tie the answer to disclosed purposes and consumer reasonable expectations.
Avoid saying California uses GDPR terminology unless comparing frameworks.
comparative_hooks: PIPA purpose limitation and minimum collection

topics.title: Service Provider, Contractor, and Third Party Contracts
topics.summary: Contract requirements, restrictions, and downstream processing duties for service providers and contractors.
answer_cautions: Do not assume a vendor is a service provider without the required contractual restrictions.
Marketing cooperative disclosures may implicate sale analysis.

topics.title: Data Breach and CCPA Private Right of Action
topics.summary: Reasonable security, California breach-notice statutes, and the limited CCPA private right of action.
answer_cautions: The CCPA private right of action is narrower than general CCPA violations.
Federal district court decisions are persuasive, not California appellate precedent.

topics.title: Risk Assessments
topics.summary: When a business must conduct risk assessments and what the assessment must include under 2026 CPPA regulations.
answer_cautions: Use the 2026 effective regulation text and retrieved date.
Do not overstate older CCPA materials as containing the 2026 risk-assessment details.

topics.title: Automated Decisionmaking Technology
topics.summary: ADMT notices, opt-out/access rights, appeals, and exceptions under the 2026 CPPA regulations.
answer_cautions: The ADMT rules are part of the 2026 regulation package.
Check exceptions before saying opt-out is always required.

topics.title: Minors and Children
topics.summary: CCPA opt-in rules for minors, child-directed enforcement, SOPIPA, and Age-Appropriate Design Code issues.
answer_cautions: Age-Appropriate Design Code obligations require current litigation/injunction status checks.
COPPA may also apply but is not part of this California KB.

topics.title: CIPA Website Tracking, Chat, and Session Replay
topics.summary: California Invasion of Privacy Act issues around wiretapping, eavesdropping, call recording, chat widgets, pixels, and session replay.
answer_cautions: CIPA is adjacent California privacy law, not CCPA.
Publication and authority level vary sharply between California Supreme Court, Ninth Circuit memorandum, and district court orders.

topics.title: CIPA Pen Register, Pixels, Cookies, and IP Address Tracking
topics.summary: California CIPA pen-register/trap-and-trace theories involving pixels, cookies, IP addresses, tracking scripts, and ad-tech routing data.
answer_cautions: CIPA pen-register cases are fast-moving and often federal district court persuasive authority.
Separate section 631 wiretap/eavesdropping theories from sections 638.50 and 638.51 pen-register theories.
comparative_hooks: PIPA behavioral advertising and tracking consent

topics.title: Data Brokers and Delete Act
topics.summary: California data broker registration and deletion mechanism provisions collected from LegInfo.
answer_cautions: Do not collapse data broker duties into ordinary CCPA business duties.
Check current CPPA rulemaking and implementation status for operational details.
comparative_hooks: PIPA personal information file registration/comparable governance

topics.title: OAG/CPPA Enforcement Authority and Administrative Materials
topics.summary: Civil and administrative enforcement powers, investigation rules, and how to use enforcement materials as nonprecedential authority.
answer_cautions: Administrative/enforcement material is not judicial precedent.
Distinguish press releases from complaints, stipulated judgments, and final agency orders.
comparative_hooks: PIPA regulator corrective orders and penalties
